title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · Changed-Lifetime Lab
summary: An independent maintainer ports the object lifetime and address ledger to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of object lifetime and address ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Object Value Identity
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 5433. Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Object Value Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Object Value Identity, keep Object Value Identity connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.
